22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis
For functioning numbers:
INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams.
For affected numbers:
INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”.
22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer
Good day
We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC.
However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally.
Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions?
Kind regards
22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response
Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers.
The affected numbers belong to this new range.
23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review
Comparison with document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3”
Section regarding:
• Normalization of incoming numbers
• E.164 format
• Translation rules on SBC
• Voice routing policies
Finding:
New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table.
Routing rule covers only the old number range.
24.09.25 – Test
Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range.
Test call performed.
Call correctly signaled and delivered.
Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible.
25.09.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated.
Internal documentation had not yet been updated.
26.09.25 – Adjustment
Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
29.09.25 – Customer feedback
All tested numbers are working again.
Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls.
Thank you for the support.
29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 22
The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

03.06.25 07:45
Phone report.
Issue:

502 – Bad Gateway

03.06.25 07:52
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 29
Status: In Progress
Notes
03.06.25 08:30
Helpdesk checks:

• Certificate valid

• Internal backend (app server) reachable

External → 502
Internal → works

03.06.25 09:10

03.06.25 10:25

• Rule for port 443 present
• NAT correct
• No blocking visible in logs
Load balancer status:
Backend server marked as “Healthy”.
03.06.25 11:40
Test with direct IP to Reverse Proxy → works.
Test via FQDN → 502
Notable:
Header “X-Forwarded-Proto” missing in external request.
04.06.25 09:05
Customer asks whether certificate may be incorrectly bound.
Certificate validation:
• CN correct
• SAN correct
• Chain complete
No obvious errors.
05.06.25 08:50
Ticket forwarded to Network / Security Team.

06.06.25 09:30
Taken over by System Engineer 21

• Policy active
• TLS inspection active
• HTTP/2 enabled
Notable:
After patch, default profile was reloaded.
06.06.25 11:15
Test: TLS inspection temporarily disabled →
Login works.
Inspection re-enabled →
502 again.
09.06.25 10:05
Further analysis:
App server expects header:
X-Forwarded-Proto: https
When TLS inspection is active, header is not forwarded.
09.06.25 14:40
Comparison with architecture documentation performed.
Reference in internal document:
“Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2”
Section 4.3 describes special header forwarding in case of TLS offloading.
10.06.25 08:25
Blueprint document loaded from DMS.
Relevant notes:
• TLS termination must occur on WAF
• Backend expects HTTPS signaling
• Custom header rewrite rule required
• Inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers
• Specific exception defined for portal application
10.06.25 11:45
Configuration comparison with blueprint shows:
• Custom header rewrite rule missing
• HTTP profile does not match documented version
• Inspection template “Default-Strict” active instead of “Portal-App-Template”
12.06.25
Internal coordination:
Firewall patch overwrote template.
Custom policy was not reassigned.
16.06.25
Configuration reviewed and adjusted according to blueprint.
No technical details documented in ticket (security policy).
18.06.25 14:55
External access test successful.
Multiple login attempts tested.
Monitoring stable for 24 hours.
Ticket closure
18.06.25 15:33
Dear Contact Person 19
The root cause was a deviation in the Reverse Proxy configuration from the documented architectural specification following a security patch.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal blueprint documentation.
The customer portal is now fully accessible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The indexed knowledge set documents the kinds of root causes and the exact remediation steps that were effective in prior, similar incidents (examples and remediation recorded above). However, those records do NOT contain your site’s live logs, firewall diffs, SBC traces/pcaps, Exchange Event Log exports or the print‑server crash dumps needed to prove the root cause for your environment. Therefore I cannot conclusively declare a single change as the universal root cause from the indexed corpus — the records show multiple distinct change events (firewall/WAF edits, NAT/session timeout adjustments, tenant policy changes, SBC translation omissions, Windows update + VM restore) that in prior tickets caused different subsets of your symptoms

Ticket number: TI-1594408
Company: Customer Company 40 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 19
Owner: Employee 29
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident

Closure
Closure date: 18.06.25 15:33
Closure text: Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to architectural specifications. Function restored.
Activities
03.06.25 07:45
Phone report.
Issue:
External users can no longer log in to the customer portal.
URL:
https://portal.kf40.ch
Browser error message:
502 – Bad Gateway
Internally (via internal network) login works.
Priority: High (customers affected)
03.06.25 07:52
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 29
Status: In Progress
Notes
03.06.25 08:30
Helpdesk checks:
• External DNS resolution correct
• Certificate valid
• HTTPS reachable
• Internal backend (app server) reachable
Test:
External → 502
Internal → works
Suspicion: Reverse Proxy / WAF
03.06.25 09:10
Customer adds:
A new certificate was installed over the weekend.
Additionally, there was a firewall update (security patch).
03.06.25 10:25
Helpdesk checks firewall:
• Rule for port 443 present
• NAT correct
• No blocking visible in logs
Load balancer status:
Backend server marked as “Healthy”.
03.06.25 11:40
Test with direct IP to Reverse Proxy → works.
Test via FQDN → 502
Notable:
Header “X-Forwarded-Proto” missing in external request.
04.06.25 09:05
Customer asks whether certificate may be incorrectly bound.
Certificate validation:
• CN correct
• SAN correct
• Chain complete
No obvious errors.
05.06.25 08:50
Ticket forwarded to Network / Security Team.
Assigned group changed to: BU Network & Security
06.06.25 09:30
Taken over by System Engineer 21
WAF analysis:
• Policy active
• TLS inspection active
• HTTP/2 enabled
Notable:
After patch, default profile was reloaded.
06.06.25 11:15
Test: TLS inspection temporarily disabled →
Login works.
Inspection re-enabled →
502 again.
09.06.25 10:05
Further analysis:
App server expects header:
X-Forwarded-Proto: https
When TLS inspection is active, header is not forwarded.
09.06.25 14:40
Comparison with architecture documentation performed.
Reference in internal document:
“Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2”
Section 4.3 describes special header forwarding in case of TLS offloading.
10.06.25 08:25
Blueprint document loaded from DMS.
Relevant notes:
• TLS termination must occur on WAF
• Backend expects HTTPS signaling
• Custom header rewrite rule required
• Inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers
• Specific exception defined for portal application
10.06.25 11:45
Configuration comparison with blueprint shows:
• Custom header rewrite rule missing
• HTTP profile does not match documented version
• Inspection template “Default-Strict” active instead of “Portal-App-Template”
12.06.25
Internal coordination:
Firewall patch overwrote template.
Custom policy was not reassigned.
16.06.25
Configuration reviewed and adjusted according to blueprint.
No technical details documented in ticket (security policy).
18.06.25 14:55
External access test successful.
Multiple login attempts tested.
Monitoring stable for 24 hours.
Ticket closure
18.06.25 15:33
Dear Contact Person 19
The root cause was a deviation in the Reverse Proxy configuration from the documented architectural specification following a security patch.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal blueprint documentation.
The customer portal is now fully accessible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Internal Teams calls are not affected.

Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry

22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day

Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.

No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received
Ticket number: TI-1612036
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 22
Owner: Employee 41
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.09.25 08:56
Closure
Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18
Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again.
Activities
18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22
Good morning
We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us.
Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds.
Other external calls are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
18.09.25 08:56
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41.
18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Could you please provide the following information:
– Example phone numbers where the issue occurs
– Since when the issue has existed
– Whether all users are affected or only specific ones
Thank you.
18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response
Here are two examples:
+41 44 785 23 10
+41 44 785 23 11
Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue.
The problem has existed since last week.
All employees are affected.
18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works.
Test with number in specified range → call not delivered.
In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown.
19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis
Possible provider issue.
Carrier contacted.
19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback
From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC.
No routing errors visible on their side.
22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis
For functioning numbers:
INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams.
For affected numbers:
INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”.
22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer
Good day
We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC.
However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally.
Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions?
Kind regards
22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response
Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers.
The affected numbers belong to this new range.
23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review
Comparison with document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3”
Section regarding:
• Normalization of incoming numbers
• E.164 format
• Translation rules on SBC
• Voice routing policies
Finding:
New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table.
Routing rule covers only the old number range.
24.09.25 – Test
Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range.
Test call performed.
Call correctly signaled and delivered.
Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible.
25.09.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated.
Internal documentation had not yet been updated.
26.09.25 – Adjustment
Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
29.09.25 – Customer feedback
All tested numbers are working again.
Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls.
Thank you for the support.
29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 22
The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls without audio (only silence)
Ticket number: TI-1602241
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 14
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 08.07.25 08:21
Closure
Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05S
Closure text: Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again.
Activities
08.07.25 08:10
Phone notification.
Several employees report:
• External calls can be established
• The other party can hear them
• They themselves hear nothing (complete silence)
Internal Teams-to-Teams calls function.
Chat and video also function.
Affected: approx. 12 users at the Zurich location.
08.07.25 08:21
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 33
Status: In Progress
Notes
08.07.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external PSTN calls?
• Only incoming or also outgoing?
• Headset or speaker?
• WLAN or LAN?
08.07.25 09:30
Customer responds:
• Only external calls affected
• Incoming and outgoing
• Various headsets
• LAN connection
• Other locations not affected
08.07.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests with affected user:
Test 1 – Internal Teams call → OK
Test 2 – External mobile call → No audio
Test 3 – External landline call → No audio
Notable:
Call status shows “Connected”.
08.07.25 11:40
Initial hypothesis: Client issue
Measures:
• Teams cache cleared
• Teams reinstalled
• Drivers checked
Result: No change.
09.07.25 08:55
Customer adds:
A new firewall rule was implemented at the location over the weekend (Security Hardening).
Unclear whether there is a correlation.
09.07.25 10:10
Helpdesk checks:
• Internet stable
• No packet loss visible
• Microsoft 365 status normal
Suspicion: Network / VoIP layer
Ticket forwarded to: BU Unified Communications
09.07.25 10:22
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 18
In-depth analysis
09.07.25 11:15
Analysis in Teams Admin Center:
• Direct Routing active
• SBC status: Online
• PSTN calls are signaled
• No errors in SIP signaling
Notable:
• RTP stream appears not to run bidirectionally
10.07.25 08:40
SBC log analysis:
• SIP INVITE correct
• 200 OK correct
• Media negotiation (SDP) successful
RTP stream from Microsoft IP visible
RTP stream from internal client not consistently visible
10.07.25 10:50
Firewall logs reviewed.
New rule implemented:
Outbound traffic restricted
Only defined port ranges allowed
Documentation shows:
Teams media ports UDP 3478–3481
and dynamic RTP ports 49152–53247
Firewall rule allows only 3478–3481.
11.07.25 09:15
Test:
Temporary opening of wider UDP port range
→ Audio works immediately.
Port restricted again
→ No audio.
11.07.25 13:20
Comparison with internal architecture documentation:
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Section 3 describes:
• Media Bypass behavior
• RTP port range requirements
• Location-specific firewall rules
• NAT traversal
14.07.25
Comparison shows:
Zurich location uses Media Bypass
Other locations do not.
With Media Bypass, RTP runs directly from client to SBC.
Firewall rule was designed only for signaling.
16.07.25
Configuration adjustment carried out according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in the ticket for security reasons.
18.07.25 15:30
Multiple external test calls successful.
Audio stable in both directions.
Monitoring 48h without anomalies.
Ticket closure
18.07.25 16:05
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media port communication following a firewall adjustment at the Zurich location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Voice transmission is stable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

18.12.24 00:33 Ticket closed | TI-1385257
Dear Employee 14,
Your ticket from 26.11.24 10:07 has been closed.
Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Notes:
26.11.24 Ticket opened by Employee 16.
26.11.24 Ticket assigned
